[ 00 ] GEOSCIENCE × MACHINE INTELLIGENCE

The Earth is amazing.

… and what it needs now more than anything is innovation.
Terrescient crafts AI-driven, environmentally-aware geoscientific solutions for resource exploration - helping drive the transition from fossil-fuel dependence to a reliable, sustainable and clean energy future.

Discover our work Get in touch →
CURRENTLY RECONSTRUCTING EARTH YEAR
-4,540,000,000

"Somewhere, something incredible is waiting to be known."

- CARL SAGAN
[ 01 ] SOFTWARE × tools × analysis

BLACKBIRD tectonics

ADVANCED PLATE TECTONIC ANALYSIS FRAMEWORK

Plate tectonic models are a critical yet complex component of the exploration and resource engineering pipeline. BLACKBIRD is an advanced proprietary software for plate-model evaluation, comparison and data-driven modelling - built directly from current academic research.

Get in contact to find out how we explore the complete solution space of statistically viable, data-driven configurations using our MODEL GENERATOR.

LOADING... REFERENCE MODEL: MÜLLER ET AL. (2022)
ESTABLISHING LINK TO PLATE MODEL DATA...
01
Calculate model uncertainty
Spatio-temporal confidence & uncertainty quantified across both inter-model and intra-model dimensions.
02
Quantify model fit-to-data
Evaluate fit to a wide range of geodynamic constraints and observational data.
03
Data-driven predictive modelling
Generate & explore the full tectonic plate-model evolution solution space.
04
Directly compare plate models
Apples-with-apples spatio-temporal comparison between different models.
05
Reconstruct plate histories
Back-calculate tectonic evolution from present-day geological and geophysical observations.
06
Integrate multi-source datasets
Fuse geophysical, geochemical and geodetic data into a unified model framework.
GPlatesPro
COMING SOON
We are proud to be working together with the EarthByte Group at The University of Sydney to develop the first commercial licence for GPlates - the open-source market-leading platform for tectonic model development used across research and industry worldwide. GPlatesPro expands the GPlates ecosystem with seamless integration into professional industry tools, bringing cutting-edge plate model visualisation and analysis to industry workflows for the first time.
Developed in collaboration with the EarthByte Group, University of Sydney - creators of the GPlates open-source project.
KEY MILESTONES
▸ First commercial GPlates licence
▸ Industry tool integration
▸ Expanding the GPlates ecosystem
▸ Built on 20+ years of research
GPLATES.ORG → EARTHBYTE.ORG →

"Any sufficiently advanced technology is indistinguishable from magic."

- ARTHUR C. CLARKE
[ 02 ] RESEARCH & DEVELOPMENT SOLUTIONS

A snapshot of what we do

06 CAPABILITIES
001
Critical mineral discovery
A data-science framework to predict, map and identify subsurface critical mineral resources for a cleaner energy future.
002
AI mapping technology
Rapidly process, data-mine and map remote-sensing data products using our AI mapping framework.
003
Space exploration
Explore mineral resource maps of both Mars and the Moon using our AI satellite mapping suite.
004
Scientific data mining
Analyse and mine existing scientific datasets to surface hidden value - turning legacy data archives into actionable geoscientific insight.
005
Digital Earth 4D modelling
Unprecedented insight through 3 billion years of geological, tectonic and geodynamic history.
006
Research consultancy
Bespoke geoscience R&D, machine learning, big data and proof-of-concept progression.

"Art is I; science is we."

- CLAUDE BERNARD
[ 03 ] COLLABORATION

Science does not happen in a vacuum. Actively working with and supporting open-access academic research is key to innovation. A selection of the global institutions and research groups we proudly collaborate with:

University of Sydney
EarthByte / School of Geosciences
ENS Paris
Laboratoire de Géologie
UT Austin - UTIG
Geodynamics & Tectonics
Curtin University
Earth Dynamics Research
University of Helsinki
& GTK Finland - Palaeomagnetics
GFZ Potsdam
Lithospheric Dynamics
Université Côte d'Azur
Laboratoire Géoazur
University of Miami
Department of Marine Geosciences

- EPICTETUS
